title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has responded to a 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining its plans to address the issues raised. The response is intended to set standards and guidelines for artificial intelligence to ensure it benefits society and maintains public trust.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: The document assesses the copyright implications of using existing works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It examines the potential impact of AI on copyright laws and vice versa.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a policy on the use of generative AI in education, outlining its stance and guidelines. This policy aims to ensure responsible and beneficial use of generative AI in educational settings.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines a plan for the Department for Transport to harness the benefits of artificial intelligence in the transport sector while addressing potential risks. It aims to guide collaboration between the DfT and the transport sector to make the most of AI's potential.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document presents a progress report on the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems as required by law. It provides an update on the situation according to the applicable regulations.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council on how to regulate AI used in medical devices. This response aims to establish clear guidelines for the development and use of AI in healthcare.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. It is a response to government requests to address AI's impact on the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government is responding to the House of Lords Select Committee's concerns about artificial intelligence by outlining its plans to address these issues. The government aims to address complacency and ensure AI is developed and used safely in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing how artificial intelligence aligns with public standards. It's likely meant to guide a review process examining the intersection of AI and public expectations or requirements.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Centre for Social Justice and Policy (CSPL) has shared its thoughts on how to regulate artificial intelligence. The submission is part of a government-organized inquiry into AI governance.
